notes:
strenges:

* Mana Break damage scales multiplicatively with enemy mana pools
* Blink enables unmatched mobility, projectile disjointing, and repositioning
* Counterspell stacks multiplicatively with base magic resistance and can reflect enemy target spells back at them
* Mana Void deals AoE damage proportional to missing mana, with flat 1.0 damage per missing mana at all ranks enabling earlier kill potential
* Illusions benefit from Mana Break and Counterspell replication, though illusion Mana Break effectiveness reduced to 25%
* Aghanim's Scepter adds +1.5% Max Mana Burned per hit, amplifying Mana Break scaling against high-mana targets
* Blink cooldown improved at early ranks (10.5/9/7.5/6s), increasing mid-game mobility uptime

weaknes:

* Completely ineffective against manaless enemies as primary damage source becomes irrelevant
* Early game extremely vulnerable with low health pool and melee attack range limitations
* Positioning dependent - must commit to melee range and becomes vulnerable when Blink is on cooldown
* Susceptible to physical damage as Counterspell only provides magic defense, with magic resistance reduced to 14/21/28/35%
* Mana Void requires team coordination and enemy mana depletion before dealing meaningful damage
* Mana Void cooldown punishing at early ranks (100/85/70s), limiting kill windows before level 3 ultimate
* Mana Void radius reduced at early ranks (400/450/500), requiring tighter positioning for AoE execution
* Illusion Mana Break effectiveness halved to 25%, significantly reducing split-push and teamfight burn output

core_mecanics:

* Mana manipulation through burning, resistance, and execution based on enemy mana depletion
* Blink-based mobility framework for positioning, escape, and initiation with flattened range curve (875/950/1025/1100)
* Illusion synergy that amplifies damage through multiple mana-burning attackers, though at reduced 25% effectiveness
* Timing-dependent power curve from weak early farming carry to dominant late game executioner
* Persecutor provides linearly scaling movement slow (12%+0.5%/level min, 24%+1%/level max), independent of Mana Void leveling

strenges:

* Forces enemy action through taunt mechanics, bypassing opponent agency and positioning completely
* Pure damage sources that ignore damage reduction, armor, and defensive modifiers
* Execute capability that bypasses death prevention effects and resets cooldown on successful kills
* Permanent power scaling through armor accumulation per Culling Blade hero kill, with stacks scaling by ability level (1/1.5/2 armor)
* Damage multiplier synergy: forced attacks trigger AoE punishment which can be combined with damage reflection
* Solo fighting power via One Man Army innate: converts 50% of armor into bonus Strength when no allied heroes are within 700 radius

weaknes:

* Completely mobility-dependent - has no built-in gap closer or escape mechanism
* Reactive damage only - Counter Helix requires enemies to attack Axe to trigger, useless against ranged kiting
* High mana burden relative to small mana pool and low intelligence growth
* Initiation exposure - Berserker's Call commits Axe without immediate damage payoff
* One Man Army requires isolation from allies to activate, creating tension between solo aggression and teamfight participation

core_mecanics:

* Berserker's Call + Counter Helix combo: force enemies to attack, guaranteeing AoE pure damage procs during taunt window
* Culling Blade execution: instantly kill targets below threshold, reset cooldown on kill, provide ally speed and armor buffs; each hero kill grants permanent armor stacks (1/1.5/2 per ability level)
* Item-dependent initiation: requires Blink Dagger to effectively position for taunts and bypass enemy frontlines
* Blade Mail synergy during taunts: reflects damage taken to attackers while Counter Helix triggers, creating triple damage source
* One Man Army innate: passively converts 50% of Axe's armor into bonus Strength when no allied heroes are within 700 radius; effect fades over 3s after an ally enters range

strenges:

* Pure damage on Brain Sap and Fiend's Grip pierces all damage reduction and immunity
* Exceptional single-target disable chain providing extended lockdown potential
* Reliable self-sustain through Brain Sap healing equal to damage dealt
* Mana drain on Fiend's Grip devastates mana-dependent targets
* Vision manipulation capabilities (Nightmare reduces vision, Fiend's Grip provides True Sight)
* Ichor of Nyctasha permanently stacks status resistance reduction against Bane's debuffs on killed heroes, making repeated kills snowball lockdown duration
* Nightmare sleepwalk repositions enemies, enabling setups into cliffs, towers, or allied abilities

weaknes:

* Fiend's Grip is a channeled ability vulnerable to any interruption
* High mana dependency on all abilities limits ability to spam spells
* No built-in mobility spells with below-average movement speed
* Lacks AoE disables and peel, struggles against multiple attackers
* Relies on sustained damage over time, incapable of burst kills
* Reduced attribute gains across the board weaken late-game scaling and durability

core_mecanics:

* Nightmare transfers to attackers
* Nightmare is a unit vector target spell; sleeping units sleepwalk in the chosen direction at 110 speed (alt-cast disables sleepwalking)
* Fiend's Grip pierces debuff immunity while draining max mana throughout the channel
* Brain Sap deals pure damage that cannot be reduced, healing Bane for the full amount
* Enfeeble severely reduces attack damage while dealing pure damage over time
* Ichor of Nyctasha applies a permanent Terror stack when an enemy hero dies under any Bane debuff or is killed by Bane, reducing their status resistance to Bane's debuffs by 5% per stack (max 5 stacks per hero)

strenges:

* Thirst removes movement speed cap completely, allowing potentially infinite speed
* Percentage-based damage and healing scales infinitely with enemy health pools
* Global True Sight vision on low health enemies provides built-in detection system
* Rupture pure damage ignores standard damage mitigation
* Bloodrage with Aghanim's Shard pierces Debuff Immunity, allowing max-health pure damage through spell immunity

weaknes:

* Significant HP costs (bloodrage) create constant vulnerability
* Combat effectiveness heavily dependent on enemies being below specific health thresholds
* Core mechanics (rupture, bloodrite) require prediction and have long counterplay windows
* Abilities are nullified by enemies who can stand still
* Sanguivore healing scales slower per level (1.5% per level), reducing mid-game sustain

core_mecanics:

* Movement-based gameplay where speed dictates positioning and chase potential
* HP management through bloodrage sacrifice and bloodmist drain for enhanced abilities
* Bloodrage is now a self-cast no-target ability affecting only Bloodseeker
* Delayed area control (bloodrite) forcing enemy positioning decisions
* Movement punishment (rupture) that forces enemies to choose between taking damage or being stationary
* Percentage-based interactions that scale with game duration rather than items

strenges:

* Global mana regeneration provides consistent team-wide resource generation across entire map
* Massive AoE magical damage potential in teamfights with slow field coverage
* Instant projectile disjoint and backward slide for emergency escape(aghanims shard)
* Glacial Guard innate converts mana spending into stacking physical barriers, rewarding frequent ability usage with added durability
* Arcane Aura mana regen amplification scaling up to 80% significantly boosts Crystal Maiden's personal mana economy

weaknes:

* Base survivability improved by Glacial Guard barriers but still fundamentally fragile without active spell casting
* Lacks innate mobility and escape abilities
* Ultimate channeling completely interrupted by any stun, silence, or disable
* Entirely magical damage provides zero impact against spell immunity or magic resistance stacking

core_mecanics:

* Survival maintains global mana regeneration for team; death removes all utility
* Root disables allow precise follow-up setup for teammates and AoE abilities
* AoE damage always centers on Crystal Maiden's current position
* Scepter transforms ultimate from stationary commitment to mobile damage zone
* Damage effectiveness heavily dependent on magic resistance manipulation from items or teammates
* Glacial Guard barriers stack from each ability cast with independent 8s durations; barrier scales at 30% + 2% per level of mana spent, enhanced by level 20 talent

strenges:

* Precision Aura provides a flat 10% agility bonus to all ranged allies independent of other abilities, offering consistent value from the start
* Gust combines knockback, silence, True Sight, and self mobility into one versatile control ability
* Marksmanship ignores enemy base armor to significantly amplify damage output against armored targets
* Multishot enables high range farming and multi-target teamfight damage while applying attack modifiers, and allows movement and item usage during channeling
* Glacier creates high ground terrain advantage for the entire team with bonus attack range, improved vision, and a 25% damage bonus for Drow when attacking from high ground
* Frost Arrows with Aghanim's Scepter reduce incoming healing on targets, adding anti-sustain utility

weaknes:

* Marksmanship armor penetration disabled when enemy heroes are in proximity, forcing spacing and positioning demands
* Multishot slows Drow Ranger's movement by 35% while casting, still leaving her partially vulnerable to crowd control
* Lacks built-in escape mobility, becoming easy prey for ganks if slowed or rooted
* Fragile early game with low base health, armor, and reduced base damage, requiring items for survivability
* Precision Aura provides no benefit to melee allies, limiting team composition flexibility

core\_mecanics:

* Positioning-based playstyle where maintaining distance from enemies unlocks Marksmanship and high ground bonuses
* Kiting-focused DPS relying on Frost Arrows slow and ranged advantage to deal damage while avoiding retaliation
* Farming presence using Multishot to participate in fights and clear waves while maintaining partial mobility
* Terrain manipulation through Glacier to create advantageous positioning and amplified damage output in team fights
* Team aura optimization where item choices and team composition amplify shared agility benefits

strenges:

* Terrain manipulation via Fissure creates impassable barriers that can isolate enemies, cut off escape routes, and control map movement
* Exponential damage scaling with Echo Slam through echo mechanics where each hit enemy generates additional damage to all nearby targets, with faster shockwave projectiles (650 speed) making damage harder to evade
* Chain crowd control through Aftershock triggering on all spell casts with an expanded 350 radius, enabling repeated AOE stuns when chaining abilities
* Mobility transformation with Aghanim's Scepter, converting Enchant Totem into a long-range repositioning tool for perfect initiation
* Passive kill pressure from Slugger, creating additional damage projectiles from killed enemies, now scaling independently per hero level
* Strong early lockdown with Fissure base stun starting at 1.0s and scaling up to 1.6s

weaknes:

* Heavy mana dependency on all core abilities requires mana management items to function in prolonged fights
* Long cast animations and cooldowns leave extended windows of vulnerability and punish poor positioning
* Echo Slam damage completely reliant on enemy clustering, dealing minimal damage against single targets
* Melee range with no innate mobility makes initiation item-dependent and vulnerable to kiting
* Fissure can trap allies and block teammate movement, creating potential for game-losing mistakes
* All crowd control do not pierces through immunity, allowing enemies to cleanse stuns with BKB
* Slugger no longer scales with Echo Slam, reducing late-game burst synergy between the two abilities

core_mecanics:

* Fissure + Enchant Totem + Echo Slam combo sequence: create terrain barrier to cluster enemies, position aggressively, unleash massive AOE burst with chain stuns
* Echo Slam exponential scaling mechanic: each enemy hit generates echo waves that damage all nearby units, with heroes counting double for echo calculation; shockwave projectile speed of 650 ensures faster damage delivery
* Aftershock as passive AOE stun trigger: automatically applies AOE stun and damage in a 350 radius on every spell cast, enabling chain disable through ability spam
* Slugger scales with hero level (27 + 3 per level on creep death, 135 + 15 per level on hero death) independently of ability skill points
* Terrain-based positioning requiring constant awareness of ally positions, cluster detection, and map geometry to maximize Fissure utility without harming own team
* Mana-gated burst playstyle where resources must be carefully managed to ensure full combo execution during critical moments

strenges:

* Blade Fury provides magic immunity, and dispels debuffs while dealing AoE damage
* Omnislash grants invulnerability and unselectability while executing targeted attacks that proc all on-hit effects
* Blade Dance critical strikes significantly amplify sustained damage output with pseudo-random reliability
* Healing Ward provides percentage-based AoE regeneration to sustain allies in fights; Aghanim's Shard increases healing and durability
* High move speed and defensive tools allow strong early game presence and split-pushing potential
* Bladeform innate grants escalating agility and movement speed bonuses while avoiding damage, rewarding careful positioning and disengagement

weaknes:

* Blade Fury negates attack damage, rendering lifesteal, crit, and cleave ineffective during its duration
* Short attack range and limited Omnislash target options restrict positioning and initiation
* High mana costs and long cooldowns on core abilities make ability usage punishing if mis-timed
* Healing Ward is extremely fragile with minimal health and easily destroyed in fights
* Cannot cast Blade Fury during Omnislash or vice versa, creating ability usage conflicts
* Bladeform stacks are lost quickly upon taking any damage, making him vulnerable to damage-over-time effects and chip damage that deny his agility and speed bonuses

core_mecanics:

* Bladeform (Innate): Gains a stack every 2s without taking damage (max 10), each granting 2.5% + 0.05% per level base Agility and 1% movement speed; all stacks fade 2s after taking any damage
* Blade Fury: Area-of-effect magical damage with magic immunity and debuff dispel; attack damage is disabled during duration
* Omnislash: Series of targeted instantaneous attacks that provide invulnerability and can execute abilities/items between jumps
* Blade Dance: Critical strike mechanic using pseudo-random distribution; critical strikes proc at attack point rather than on damage
* Healing Ward: Mobile aura providing percentage-based health regeneration; Aghanim's Shard increases healing by 1.5% and adds 1 hit to destroy
* Swiftslash (Aghanim's Scepter): Shorter duration Omnislash variant for cleanup scenarios on cooldown reduction

strenges:

* Long-range stun initiation with travel-time scaling and vision provision
* Reliable area damage with guaranteed secondary target on closest enemy
* Multi-charge leap system for high mobility and repositioning
* Global team invisibility for coordinated attacks and escapes
* Celestial Quiver innate provides scaling magic damage on attacks, amplified by Leap charges

weaknes:

* Highly mana-dependent with small mana pool limiting spellcasting
* Physically frail with low base health and no built-in sustain
* Invisibility activation too slow for reactionary escapes
* Arrow landing zones ineffective at close range due to minimum stun
* Long ulti cooldowns require careful usage planning
* Celestial Quiver charge count is limited early, reducing sustained trade potential

core_mecanics:

* Sacred Arrow stun duration and damage scale with distance traveled
* Starstorm's double-hit mechanic guarantees secondary meteor on nearest target
* Leap charges recharge over time, with buffs applying instantly on cast
* Moonlight Shadow requires pre-casting due to fade delay, invisibility reapplies if revealed
* High-kit synergy but requires forward planning due to mobility and commitment trades
* Celestial Quiver is an auto-cast attack modifier dealing 3 magic damage per Mirana's level, starting with 2 max charges and gaining 1 more every 7 levels on a 6s restore time
* Aghanim's Shard grants 3 temporary Celestial Quiver charges on Leap cast, stacking with subsequent Leaps and ignoring the max charge cap

strenges:

* Dynamic attribute redistribution allows adaptation between pure damage output and exceptional survivability
* Waveform provides invulnerable mobility for initiation, escape, and wave-clearing
* Ability to copy enemy heroes grants access to their basic abilities for strategic versatility
* Strong late-game scaling as an agility carry with flexible stat allocation
* Ebb and Flow innate grants scaling utility from both attributes — Agility boosts movement speed and attack range, Strength boosts cast range and slow resistance, rewarding frequent attribute shifting
* Adaptive Strike deals higher base damage at all ranks, improving burst and farming in the early-to-mid game

weaknes:

* Micro-intensive attribute management requires constant attention and precise decision-making under pressure
* Highly vulnerable when Waveform is on cooldown, creating significant cooldown-dependent windows of weakness; reduced max range at later ranks narrows escape distance
* Mana-intensive playstyle limits sustainability in prolonged fights through ability costs and stat shifting
* Early-game struggles due to dependency on proper attribute management and item timing to reach power spikes

core_mecanics:

* Waveform functions as primary mobility tool with invulnerability, serving dual purpose as nuke and escape mechanism (max range 700/775/850/925)
* Attribute Shift enables real-time conversion between Agility for damage and Strength for HP pool, with mana cost per second
* Morph ability copies targeted enemy heroes, gaining their basic abilities while allowing instant switch between forms
* Ebb and Flow innate passively converts Strength into cast range (+20%) and slow resistance (+20%), and Agility into movement speed (+15%) and ranged attack range (+20%); bonuses persist while replicating except extra attack range on melee replicate targets

strenges:

* Insanely high damage potential from Necromastery stack collection and Shadowraze damage amplification
* Devastating teamfight ultimate that deals massive damage while slowing and reducing magic resistance
* Armor reduction aura that punishes enemy positioning across entire teamfight
* Shadowraze now scales with Necromastery souls (+3 damage per soul), rewarding high soul counts with massive Raze damage
* Feast of Souls rework allows soul collection without spending existing stacks, enabling aggressive use without losing Necromastery damage
* Aghanim's Scepter returning Requiem no longer has a damage penalty, significantly boosting teamfight burst

weaknes:

* Loses significant portion of damage capability upon death (soul penalty)
* Extremely fragile early game with low base health and strength
* Requires precise positioning for Shadowraze accuracy and optimal Requiem placement
* Heavily dependent on consistent farming to maintain soul stacks for effectiveness
* Requiem of Souls now capped at 20 souls per cast, limiting ultimate scaling in late game
* Lower base Shadowraze damage (85/150/215/280) makes early levels weaker before soul stacks accumulate
* Necromastery max souls reduced to flat 20 base, requiring talent investment to exceed that cap

core_mecanics:

* Necromastery passive collects souls from kills for attack damage bonuses (1.35 + 0.15 per level per soul), with severe penalty upon death. No longer levels with Requiem of Souls; base max souls is flat 20
* Three Shadowraze variants at different distances with stacking damage amplifier on hit targets, plus 3 bonus damage per Necromastery soul. Aghanim's Shard adds a stacking 12% slow debuff on enemies hit
* Requiem of Souls ultimate channels then releases lines of damaging energy with slow and magic resistance reduction, capped at 20 souls per cast. Aghanim's Scepter return wave now deals full damage
* Feast of Souls no longer consumes souls; instead temporarily collects souls from up to 4/6/8/10 nearby enemies (600 radius, 2 per 0.5s, heroes give 3 souls, creeps give 1). Souls from still-living targets are lost when the effect ends; the rest are retained for 8s. Grants reduced bonus attack speed (35/50/65/80) and movement speed (4/6/8/10%)

strenges:
- Exponential illusion growth through Juxtapose - each attack from PL or illusions can create more illusions, rapidly building overwhelming army
- Phantom Rush provides reliable gap-closing that works on all illusions, creating coordinated swarming effect
- Doppelganger offers escape, repositioning, and additional illusion generation simultaneously
- Spirit Lance combines catch potential, slow, damage, and illusion creation in one spell
- Strong natural late-game scaling - illusions benefit from agility increases through damage and attack speed

weaknes:
- Extremely vulnerable to AoE damage - entire illusion army can be eliminated quickly by splash/AoE spells
- Weak early game reliant on melee range and requires significant levels/items to become effective
- Limited illusion duration - army cannot be maintained indefinitely, requires constant combat engagement
- Mana依赖 for engagement and survival abilities
- No burst damage - relies on sustained physical damage through prolonged engagement and overwhelming numbers

core_mecanics:
- illusion multiplication through Juxtapose - pseudo-random chance creates exponential army growth with maximum illusion caps
- Phantom Rush toggle ability for gap-closing - all illusions possess this ability for coordinated swarming
- Doppelganger removes PL and nearby illusions from battlefield, gathers them, then reappears with additional illusions
- Continuous combat engagement required to maintain illusion army due to limited duration mechanics
- Teamfight positioning around building illusions while spreading them to minimize AoE exposure
- Illusory Armaments min damage increase scales at 2% per 3 hero levels rather than per 3 level-ups, providing slightly earlier damage thresholds

strenges:

* Exceptional mobility with multiple escape tools: instant teleportation to orb location, short-range blink with silence, and invulnerability dodge mechanic
* Powerful multi-purpose control through AoE root that locks enemy positioning and punishes movement with extensive stun duration
* Consistent magical burst threat with ranged AoE nuke and silence combination for ganking potential
* Faster Illusory Orb (750 speed) with persistent AoE damage-over-time zone increases area denial and zoning power

weaknes:

* Mana-intensive abilities with significant cooldown periods create severe vulnerability windows when overextended
* Extremely fragile with low durability and health pool; inability to survive burst or chain-CC without abilities available
* Effectiveness heavily positioning-dependent; core control ability requires enemies to break tether for full impact
* Puckish facet spell dodge multiplier reduced to 3x, narrowing the survivability window from passive evasion

core_mecanics:

* Illusory Orb + Ethereal Jaunt synergy provides primary mobility and escape through instant teleportation to orb position; orb now travels at 750 speed and deals 3% of Impact Damage every 0.5s in its AoE
* Phase Shift grants brief but powerful invulnerability window that can dodge any damage type and allows item usage during effect
* Dream Coil creates tether zones that limit enemy spacing, with root duration punishing movement beyond break distance
* Waning Rift combines short-range teleport, AoE damage, and silence for repositioning and disruption
* Illusory Orb now uses curved vector targeting by default, enabling arcing trajectories around obstacles; alt-cast launches the orb straight
* Facet choice alters playstyle through different orb trajectory patterns or controlled repositioning options; Puckish restores 3% health/mana with a 3x spell dodge multiplier

strenges:
- Unmatched displacement capability with hook - longest range non-ultimate isolation tool in game
- Flat strength gain per kill from Flesh Heap provides stronger early snowball potential independent of items
- Area denial through rot that controls space and punishes poor positioning
- Active damage negation to survive burst and critical abilities
- Strength-based damage scaling that grows stronger over time

weaknes:
- Extremely low mobility with no escape mechanism - completely positioning dependent
- Severe mana constraints limit hook usage and require careful resource management
- Hook requires exceptional prediction and reaction - most accuracy-dependent ability in game
- Lowest agility gain in game - poor attack speed and armor progression
- All abilities can be disrupted, baited, or played around by enemy awareness
- Flesh Heap no longer levels with Dismember - requires dedicated skill points, straining limited skill build

core_mecanics:
- Hook-based displacement as primary initiation and pick-off mechanic
- Flat passive strength accumulation (1.6 per kill) that rewards early aggression but scales less explosively into late game
- Flesh Heap is now an independent ability requiring its own skill point investment separate from Dismember
- Toggleable self-damage curse for area denial and strategic depth
- Channeled disable that converts strength into damage and healing
- Timing-dependent active damage mitigation on predictable cooldowns

strenges:
- Gains permanent movement speed scaling with hero levels
- Strong passive damage output triggered by incoming attacks
- Steals enemy attack damage to simultaneously reduce their threat and amplify own damage, with improved drain rate
- Consistent armor reduction against targets
- Dynamo facet enables attack damage to convert into spell amplification
- Aghanim's Shard doubles Storm Surge strike chance and enables AoE lightning during Eye of the Storm

weaknes:
- Must maintain close tether to enemies for peak damage output
- Limited active mobility with no escape mechanisms
- Damage output relies on being attacked to trigger passive effects
- Plasma Field effectiveness requires precise positioning relative to enemies
- Highly vulnerable when Static Link connection breaks
- Lost Spell Lifesteal talent option, reducing sustain in prolonged fights

core_mecanics:
- Plasma Field: Deploy expanding and contracting wave for AoE damage and slow
- Static Link: Establish tether that drains enemy attack damage over time (6/12/18/24 per second)
- Storm Surge: Passive lightning damage and slow procs when attacked; Shard upgrade doubles strike chance and hits all enemies in Eye of the Storm radius
- Eye of the Storm: Auto-targeting ultimate damaging lowest HP hero while reducing armor

strenges:
- Excellent mobility gap-closer with extended cast range that provides consistent initiation opportunities and escape options
- Devastating teamfight presence through powerful channeling ultimate with dual slow effects
- Multiple area-of-effect abilities for effective crowd control, zone denial, and waveclear
- Strong chain damage potential through explosive death mechanic when enemies are clustered
- Consistent utility through stuns and slows that remain valuable throughout all game stages
- Burrowstrike into Sand Storm grants immediate re-stealth, enabling safe retreats and deceptive re-engages
- Late-game talent option adds blind effect to Sand Storm, reducing enemy right-click threat in teamfights

weaknes:
- Ultimate requires uninterrupted channeling, making it highly vulnerable to stuns, silences, and disruption
- Position-dependent abilities that demand precise timing and execution for maximum effectiveness
- Low natural survivability and vulnerability during ability cooldown windows
- Struggles against mobile heroes who can escape AoE zones easily
- Reduced effectiveness against magic-immune targets who negate majority of damage output
- Caustic Finale no longer scales with Epicenter and has reduced early values and flat shorter duration, weakening early-mid game trading

core_mecanics:
- Classic Burrowstrike + Epicenter combo: engage with mobility skill, then channel ultimate for maximum damage
- Caustic Finale chains: attack clusters of enemies to trigger explosive chain reactions when they die; ability now scales independently per hero level rather than with Epicenter
- Area control with Sand Storm: create zones of damage and slow to force enemy positioning
- Mobility-based initiator: relies on gap-closer to position for devastating AoE abilities
- Blink Dagger synergy: essential positioning tool for successful combo execution
- Burrowstrike + Sand Storm stealth loop: ending Burrowstrike inside Sand Storm immediately re-grants invisibility for repeated hit-and-hide plays
- Talent progression shifts: level 20 offers additional Epicenter pulses for raw damage, level 25 choices include Sand Storm blind or bonus Stinger damage

strenges:

* Unmatched global mobility enabling cross-map positioning and safe escapes
* Sustained mana economy with passive regeneration scaling on nearby enemy deaths, though reduced by loss of Ball Lightning level-up charges
* High burst magic damage with frequent casting enabled by low cooldowns
* Strong crowd control combining enemy pulls with movement and attack speed slows
* Excellent ganking and split-pushing presence through superior positioning

weaknes:

* Extreme mana dependency makes positioning errors fatal
* Completely shut down by silence and mana drain effects
* Precise mana management required during Ball Lightning to avoid stranding
* High mechanical complexity amplifies the cost of execution mistakes
* Static Remnant travel time (300 speed) requires leading targets, reducing reliable instant burst

core_mecanics:

* Ball Lightning: directional mobility consuming mana continuously over distance traveled
* Overload: automatic damage and slow effect applied on every spell cast
* Electric Vortex: short-range channeled pull that disables enemies in place
* Static Remnant: spawns at Storm Spirit's position and travels at 300 speed to target location, delayed area explosion for wave clear and burst combos
* Galvanized: passive mana regeneration accumulating charges from enemy hero deaths; no longer gains bonus charges from leveling Ball Lightning

strenges:

* Reliable targeted stun with AoE damage provides consistent initiation and setup, further enhanced by talent options reducing Storm Hammer cooldown and mana cost
* Built-in cleave and massive damage amplification enable rapid farming and explosive teamfight potential
* Team-wide mobility buff and passive damage bonus enhance both individual and squad effectiveness; Shard upgrade makes Warcry undispellable with a 300 physical damage barrier and expanded radius
* Wrath of God innate amplifies attack damage gained per point of Strength, compounding value from stat growth and God's Strength

weaknes:

* Melee nature requires close positioning, exposing Sven to disables and damage
* No innate gap-closing or escape abilities, creating heavy dependence on mobility items
* Ultimate has significant cooldown, creating extended vulnerability windows
* Wrath of God innate is disabled by Break, significantly reducing damage output when debuffed

core_mecanics:

* Stun initiation followed by positional cleave damage on follow-up attacks for burst output
* Timed power spike through ultimate activation requiring coordination with item timing
* Strength-based scaling through Wrath of God innate, where each point of Strength grants bonus attack damage scaling with level, making stat investment contribute to both damage and survivability
* Position-dependent gameplay that rewards aggressive frontline placement with proper support

strenges:

* Massive multi-instance burst damage
* Versatile crowd control with stun and slows
* Long-range initiation capabilities
* Cleave attacks for farming and teamfight damage
* Strong scaling damage and tankiness
* Insurmountable innate grants scaling slow resistance and status resistance based on Strength
* Tree Grab scales heavily with levels: lower cooldown and more attacks at max rank

weaknes:

* Attack speed penalty reduces auto-attack DPS
* Tree Throw no longer slows by default without Aghanim's Shard
* Unreliable Toss targeting (grabs nearest unit)
* Mana issues with frequent ability usage, especially Avalanche
* Burst heavily depends on proper ability combos

core_mecanics:

* Insurmountable (innate): Slow Resistance (including Attack Speed Slows) equal to 20% of Strength, Status Resistance equal to 10% of Strength
* Avalanche: Multi-instance damage area stun
* Toss: Grabs and throws nearest unit, synergizes with Avalanche; flat 125 mana cost at all levels
* Tree Grab: Transforms attacks into ranged with bonus damage and cleave; 4/5/6/7 attacks, cooldown 15/12/9/6s
* Tree Throw/Volley: Ranged damage with splash; Tree Volley uses Tree Throw bonus damage and Shard bonuses
* Grow: Passive scaling that boosts damage/armor/movement speed but reduces attack speed; Shard adds +20% AoE damage, +125 radius, 25% move slow and 45 attack speed slow for 2.5s to Toss/Tree Throw/Tree Volley

strenges:

* Nether Swap provides instant repositioning control for saves, initiations, or setups
* Significantly amplifies ally damage output through Vengeance Aura and enemy armor reduction
* Combines vision control with strong disabling capabilities
* Versatile role flexibility as support, initiator, disabler, or escape
* Aghanim's allows you to play more aggressively due to the creation of an illusion after death that can use abilities

weaknes:

* Low survivability with no innate defensive tools and dangerous positioning requirements
* High mana dependency restricts ability usage frequency without items
* Limited mobility with Nether Swap as sole reposition option at meaningful cooldown
* Heavily positioning-dependent; one mistake can be fatal

core\_mecanics:

* Magic Missile: targeted single-target stun with damage component
* Wave of Terror: linear wave that reduces enemy armor and grants vision along path
* Vengeance Aura: passive damage amplification aura for nearby allies
* Nether Swap: instantly swaps positions with target unit, can ally-save or enemy-initiate
* Retribution: debuffs killer with damage amplification against them from Vengeful

strenges:

* Tailwind stacking movement speed from ability usage enables aggressive chaining and chase potential
* Passively maintains 600 max movement speed cap through Tailwind innate
* Windrun provides 100% evasion making immune to all auto-attacks and illusions
* Shackleshot can deliver massive stuns up to 4+ seconds when positioned behind tree or enemy
* Focus Fire grants extreme attack speed (350-500) allowing rapid on-hit effect application; with Aghanim's Scepter each attack triggers Tailwind for sustained high mobility
* Gale Force offers long-range area control that repositions enemies to set up plays
* Level 25 Powershot execute talent provides kill-securing ability against low-HP heroes

weaknes:

* No longer prevents slows — Tailwind replaced Easy Breezy's unconditional slow immunity, leaving Windranger vulnerable to movement-impairing effects
* Lower base movement speed (285) makes her more reliant on Tailwind and Windrun to stay mobile
* Shackleshot requires precise positioning with tree or enemy directly behind target, failing to short 0.6s stun otherwise
* Focus Fire reduces attack damage to 75%, limiting overall damage output and forcing single-target focus

core_mecanics:

- Passive team aura providing damage and night vision
- Multi-beam AoE ultimate requiring enemy proximity
- Night cycle map control mechanics
- Facet choices between teamfight or single-target focus
- Lunar Orbit grants 8/12/16/20% damage reduction while active; Shard adds 10% extra reduction and 20% movement speed
- Level 20 talent choice between Lunar Orbit damage/speed multiplier or alternative scaling

strenges:
- Cumulative dragon evolution system with each level adding new effects (corrosive → splash → frost), increasing power ceiling at level 3
- Melee-to-ranged transformation during fights enables versatile positioning and combat engagement
- Exceptional durability from passive HP regeneration and armor scaling, amplified in dragon form
- Strong teamfight presence from splash damage, reliable instant stun with innate AoE, and sustained frontline presence
- Massively extended Breathe Fire cast range (1000) enables safe long-range damage and reduction application
- Wyrm's Wrath bonus magic damage and AoE always active regardless of form, improving baseline effectiveness
- Elder Dragon Form grants +350 cast range on all abilities, dramatically extending engagement range

weaknes:
- Slow base attack time limits DPS output and farming efficiency
- Significant downtime between dragon transformations creates vulnerability windows
- Complete lack of mobility or escape abilities compounded by reduced base movement speed, making positioning mistakes even more punishing
- Reduced strength gain lowers late-game HP scaling and overall tankiness
- Breathe Fire damage reduction weaker in early levels (20% at rank 1 vs previous flat 30%)

core_mecanics:
- Elder Dragon Form evolves cumulatively per level: Green (corrosive poison), Red (adds splash), Blue (adds frost slow piercing debuff immunity)
- Dragon Tail has innate 25 radius AoE and no longer changes cast range based on dragon form
- Wyrm's Wrath permanently grants bonus magic damage and area of effect on attacks in all forms
- Elder Dragon Form provides scaling move speed, +350 ability cast range, and cumulative combat modifiers instead of bonus attack damage
- Aghanim's Scepter unlocks level 4 Black Dragon with enhanced stats, magic resistance, and free pathing

strenges:

- Consistent flat armor stacking through ability usage with Weave at all levels
- Invulnerable soul clone that can cast abilities independently
- AoE sustain with bouncing physical damage that overlaps and spikes
- Prevents ally death while amplifying healing on severely wounded targets
- Cone AoE poison with low-HP execute capability
- Aghanim's Shard enables Weave to heal allies per stack, scaling with accumulated stacks

weaknes:

- Tether constraints on soul projection limit positional freedom
- Extremely mana-hungry abilities drain resources quickly
- Entire kit relies on physical damage, negated by high armor
- Shallow grave protects only one ally and can be purged
- Precise timing and positioning required for ability effectiveness
- Nothl Projection no longer provides hard dispel on Dazzle when ending, reducing self-purge capability
- Weave duration reduced to 6.9s base, requiring more frequent reapplication at early levels

core_mecanics:

- Flat armor stacking system (1 per stack at all levels) with duration scaling from 6.9s by 0.1s per level
- Soul separation creating invulnerable casting entity with tether mechanics, no longer dispels on return
- Bouncing cascade damage/healing that targets enemies and allies
- Death prevention with healing amplification scaling off missing HP
- Cone-based poison application with execution threshold on low-health targets
- Weave and Nothl Projection level independently of each other
- Shard upgrade grants 60 HP heal per existing Weave stack when a new stack is applied to an ally

strenges:
- Power Cogs physically contain enemies while Clockwerk moves freely through them
- Hookshot enables instant long-range targeted engagement and initiation
- Battery Assault delivers sustained mini-stuns that interrupt channels and attacks
- Rocket Flare provides global presence and True Sight
- Overclocking dramatically enhances all abilities with increased radius, armor, and Rocket Flare power
- Armor Power facet scales defensive stats by consuming Chainmails for permanent armor
- Increased Power Cogs Mana Burn punishes trapped enemies harder at all levels

weaknes:
- Missed Hookshots are extremely punishing with long base cooldown
- Predictable engagements with visible travel time allowing counterplay
- Severely countered by magic immunity
- Overclocking no longer provides attack speed inside Cogs, reducing sustained damage output

core_mecanics:
- Multi-stage initiation combo: Hookshot → automatic Cogs → Battery Assault lockdown
- Power Cogs trap enemies inside while Clockwerk passes through freely, enabling repositioning
- Rocket Flare global True Sight projectile for vision and map pressure
- Facet system with cooldown reduction and Armor Power Chainmail consumption options
- Overclocking extends duration to 18s, expands Battery Assault and Cogs radius to 330, grants 25% bonus armor near Cogs, and boosts Rocket Flare damage/vision/slow by 35%
- Talent progression offers mana sustain, Hookshot cooldown reduction, and Rocket Flare charges

strenges:
- Sustained damage output through pure damage and toggle abilities
- Strong wave clear and pushing capability with concentrated Diabolic Edict burst
- AoE crowd control
- Flexible facet system for aggressive or defensive play

weaknes:
- Positioning dependency with short-range abilities
- Extremely mana intensive
- Low mobility with no escapes
- Fragile with low base survivability

core_mecanics:
- Toggle ability providing constant area damage at mana cost
- Pure damage pulses over 8s duration, effective against buildings
- Abilities scale area-of-effect with intelligence
- AoE stun with delay and bouncing damage spell
- Ethereal transformation reducing enemy magic resistance

strenges:
- Global teleportation enables anywhere map presence for split-pushing and strategic positioning
- Arrival barrier grants immediate survivability when teleporting into hostile territory, further enhanced by talent option
- Adaptive damage scaling based on nearby trees incentivizes forest engagement, now shared by treants for compounding damage
- Terrain manipulation through Sprout and ent summons controls teamfight positioning, with improved Sprout vision
- Global ultimate provides cross-map teamfight impact through bouncing damage
- Improved base damage strengthens early laning and last-hit ability
- Treants gain free pathing through trees, magic resistance, and bonus hero damage, making them far more threatening

weaknes:
- Teleportation highly predictable with visible animation and sound through fog of war
- Core abilities limited by tree dependency and forest terrain availability
- Cooldown constraints create significant vulnerability during downtime periods
- Vulnerable to AOE damage and crowd control that clears ents, though treant magic resistance partially mitigates this
- Treants have reduced base health, making them frailer without their new magic resistance against physical damage

core_mecanics:
- Global teleportation with arrival barrier enables aggressive map movement
- Passive damage bonus based on proximity to surrounding trees, now scaling independently at 300 + 10 radius per level with 2x treant multiplier
- Treants also carry Spirit of the Forest, gaining bonus damage from nearby trees and other treants
- Sprout creates tree formations that block pathing and provide expanded vision (400 range)
- Converting trees into controlled ents with free tree pathing, 25% magic resistance, and bonus hero damage for map pressure
- Global bouncing ultimate that hits enemies across the map, no longer tied to Spirit of the Forest leveling
- Level 25 talent multiplies treant HP/damage but no longer affects Spirit of the Forest multiplier

strenges:

- Intrinsic sustainability through percent-based lifesteal and damage on attacks
- Permanent HP scaling from kills, with increasing returns as Feast is leveled
- Debuff immunity with high magic resistance providing strong survival
- Invulnerability and healing via creep consumption and infest mechanics, including Ancient creeps by default
- Strong laning presence with high base damage and attack speed
- Rage grants bonus movement speed, adding chase and escape potential during fights

weaknes:

- Mana constraints from low intelligence with high ability costs, though slightly eased by Open Wounds cost reduction
- Melee limitation with limited mobility outside of Rage's active duration
- Cooldown-dependent playstyle creating vulnerability windows
- Susceptible to pure damage, break effects, and crowd control
- Single-target focus limits effectiveness against groups
- Feast's weaker early scaling requires ability levels to match previous sustain values

core_mecanics:

- Ghoul Frenzy is an innate passive granting 5 bonus Attack Speed per level
- Feast is a regular ability providing percent-based sustain and permanent HP scaling (10/15/20/25 per hero kill)
- Rage grants spell immunity, magic resistance, and 9/12/15/18% bonus movement speed
- Open Wounds applies a flat 50% max slow while amplifying allied lifesteal, with reduced mana cost
- Infest offers invulnerability, healing, stat boost to host, and works on Ancient creeps by default; Shard adds 30% remaining HP as DOT when consuming creeps
- Passive mobility and attack speed enhance farming efficiency and fighting capability

strenges:
- Exceptional teamfight control via Vacuum positioning manipulation and Wall of Replica illusions
- Unstoppable mobility with Surge (slow immunity, phasing, max MS cap)
- Sustained area pressure with Ion Shell and pickoff potential with Normal Punch
- Quick Wit grants attack speed scaling with Intelligence, boosting right-click output

weaknes:
- Mana-intensive, limiting ability uptime; Quick Wit restore reduced to 8.5%
- Requires precise Blink Dagger positioning to enable Vacuum setups
- Wall of Replica depends on enemy choices, nullified if avoided
- Highly vulnerable to silence and disruption
- Power spike delayed without key items

core_mecanics:
- Vacuum: Pulls enemies toward target point, destroys trees
- Surge: Grants max movement speed, phasing, and slow immunity; talent option for -1.5s cooldown at level 15
- Ion Shell: AoE damage aura on attached target
- Normal Punch: Movement-charged punch with stun, damage scales with distance traveled
- Wall of Replica: Creates controlled illusions from enemies passing through
- Quick Wit (formerly Aggrandize): Restores 8.5% max health/mana, grants +1 attack speed per point of Intelligence

strenges:
- Lane dominance through Searing Arrows autocast providing spell damage and safe last-hits
- Massive sustain burst from Death Pact granting temporary max HP and heal
- High mobility and safety via Skeleton Walk stealth and movement speed boost
- Exceptional burst damage potential with Strafe's attack speed and range bonus
- Superior tower damage and pushing capability
- Improved late-game survivability with buffed Death Pact talent bonus HP (400)

weaknes:
- Severe mana constraints limiting ability usage early game
- Extremely fragile without Death Pact active
- Cooldown limitations on burst and sustain windows
- No hard control - completely lacks stun or root
- Dependent on enemies lacking detection and creep availability
- Lower base movement speed (285) makes kiting and repositioning without Skeleton Walk harder

core_mecanics:
- Searing Arrows autocast for spell damage attacks that scale with items
- Skeleton Walk provides stealth, speed burst, and summons Skeleton Archers
- Strafe grants massive attack speed boost with range bonus for burst windows; skeleton attack speed multiplier at 40%
- Death Pact consumes creeps to gain temporary max HP and healing
- Infernal Shred passively stacks armor reduction on attacked targets
- Burning Army summons Skeleton Archers for DPS and pushing
- Burning Barrage channels piercing AOE damage
- Attack speed amplifies Searing Arrows damage output

strenges:
- Dual-role versatility through facets (damage vs support focus)
- Mobile physical immunity aura ultimate combined with single-target debuff immunity
- Strong burst damage-heal synergy that scales with items
- Purification and Hammer of Purity both pierce debuff immunity, threatening BKB carriers
- Hammer of Purity provides sustain through heal-over-time on damage dealt
- Repel on significantly lower cooldown enables more frequent saves
- Powerful laning presence through burst-driven play
- Guardian Angel Scepter upgrade provides global reach with building protection and heal amplification

weaknes:
- Zero mobility or escape (melee, no dash or blink)
- High mana dependency limits ability spam
- Lacks hard crowd control or lockdown
- Vulnerable to counter-picks that kite or burst through magic damage
- Reduced base armor and agility gain weaken physical durability in lane
- Guardian Angel has no linger duration, requiring Omniknight to stay near allies
- Repel lost bonus Strength and per-debuff scaling, reducing its overall value

core_mecanics:
- Purification - delayed burst that heals allies and damages nearby enemies; now pierces debuff immunity on enemies
- Hammer of Purity - autocast pure damage bonus with attack range increase; pierces debuff immunity; heals Omniknight for 30% of damage dealt over 5s
- Repel - debuff immunity shield granting 60% magic resistance and bonus health regen; no longer provides bonus Strength or per-debuff effects
- Guardian Angel - mobile AOE physical damage immunity aura centered on Omniknight (700 radius), no linger duration; Scepter makes it global, affects buildings, and amplifies health restoration by 100%
- Degen Aura - innate movement speed slow aura (11% + 1% per level), no longer levels with Guardian Angel

strenges:
- Early game neutral creep control for farm independence
- Untouchable passive provides significant survivability via attack speed slow, further amplified by level 25 talent (+80 attack slow)
- Self-sustain through healing over time to self and allies
- Impetus damage scales dramatically with attack distance, synergized by Enchant's bonus attack range against enchanted heroes
- Dominant power window in early-mid game timeframe
- Enchant grants bonus attack range against enchanted enemy heroes for Enchantress and controlled units

weaknes:
- Severely limited by mana availability and high costs
- Fragile positioning dependency despite defensive passive
- Significant power drop-off in late game
- Highly vulnerable to burst damage and lockdown
- Distance-based damage capped by maximum range limitations
- Rabble-Rouser damage scaling reduced, weakening controlled creep contribution

core_mecanics:
- Enchant charms enemy heroes or neutral creeps, applying dispel and movement slow; enchanting enemy heroes increases attack range against them by 50/100/150/200 for Enchantress and controlled units
- Enchant cast range scales from 500/600/700/800
- Untouchable reduces attackers' attack speed substantially
- Impetus deals bonus damage based on attack distance with exceptional range
- Nature's Attendants provides healing over time to self and allies via wisps
- Sproink leaps backward to disjoint projectiles and trigger Impetus attacks
- Rabble-Rouser increases damage by 4% per level for controlled creeps

strenges:
- Health-based power scaling grants massive attack speed, magic resistance, and regeneration as health decreases
- Zero mana dependency forces cost-efficient resource management
- Instant engagement capabilities with Life Break for aggressive positioning
- Sustained pressure damage through stacking Burning Spear DoT, now amplified by 0.5% enemy max health burn per stack
- Area control via Inner Fire's increased damage (110/180/250/320) and distance-scaled knockback
- Berserker's Blood Shard active provides basic dispel and delayed heal, improving survivability against debuff-heavy lineups

weaknes:
- No escape mechanism, mobility purely offensive; worsened by base move speed reduction to 290
- Extremely vulnerable to burst and percentage-based damage
- Requires precise low-health management with severe positioning risks; Burning Spear's 2% max health cost punishes usage harder at low HP thresholds
- Attack speed damage negated by enemy armor stacking
- Melee range restricts target access in teamfights
- Inner Fire knockback effectiveness reduced against distant enemies (flat 25 unit knockback at 375+ range)

core_mecanics:
- Berserker's Blood: Passive grants escalating bonuses at lower health thresholds; Shard adds an active basic dispel (30% current HP cost, 20s cooldown) with a delayed heal after 3s that scales with debuffs dispelled (3% max HP per debuff)
- Blood Magic: Zero mana pool converts all ability costs to health
- Burning Spear: Stacking DoT applied via auto-attacks at 2% max health cost per attack; additionally burns targets for 0.5% of their max health per stack
- Life Break: Instant gap closer that deals percentage current health damage
- Inner Fire: Knockback duration now scales with distance; enemies beyond 375 units receive only a minimal 25-unit knockback
- Peak effectiveness in early-mid game timing window

strenges:
- Significantly stronger during night with massive movement and attack speed bonuses
- Powerful crowd control combination with AoE slow (Void with Shard hits 400 radius) and silence
- Superior vision control and unobstructed movement during ultimate
- Strong sustain through Midnight Feast passive lifesteal and nighttime creep consume
- Aghanim's Scepter enables snowball potential with basic ability cooldown reset on hero kill

weaknes:
- Fundamentally weak during daylight hours with predictable power windows
- Lacks gap-closing abilities and hard crowd control
- Limited laning presence, though Midnight Feast passive heal improves lane survivability

core_mecanics:
- Power scales with day/night cycle, peaking during nighttime
- Void provides AoE slow with damage, Crippling Fear creates silence aura
- Hunter in the Night is now an innate ability granting movement and attack speed bonuses during darkness, scaling with hero level
- Midnight Feast passively heals on attack; at night can actively consume a non-ancient creep to restore a percentage of max health and mana

strenges:
- Superior map mobility and territory control through webs that provide terrain traversal and bonuses
- Army-scale swarm tactics with spiderling generation that applies movement slow
- Combat sustain and healing through passive heal that scales with hero level
- Strong combat steroid with damage multiplier and significant lifesteal that extends to nearby Spiderlings

weaknes:
- Web dependency severely limits mobility and combat effectiveness outside webs
- Spiderling army extremely vulnerable to AOE damage and splash attacks
- Spider base damage does not scale with items, though ultimate lifesteal partially compensates
- Short combat window during ultimate burst
- Reduced web movespeed bonuses limit roaming tempo between web clusters

core_mecanics:
- Create webs for permanent mobility, regeneration zones, and territory control
- Inject enemies to spawn spiderling armies that swarm and slow opponents
- Apply movement slow, miss chance, and bonus damage through Incapacitating Bite
- Activate Insatiable Hunger for damage and lifesteal burst during key engagements; lifesteal now shared with Spiderlings within 800 range
- Level 25 talent extends Insatiable Hunger BAT reduction to nearby Spiderlings, enabling late-game swarm DPS spike
- Peak effectiveness during mid-game timing window

strenges:
- Stealth-based gameplay enabling strategic positioning and surprise attacks
- Superior gold economy through multiple passive and active gold-generating abilities, amplified against high-net-worth targets via Big Game Hunter
- High burst damage output from ability-based attacking
- Exceptional map control through tracking and information advantage
- Enhanced mobility while invisible via Shadow Walk movement speed bonus

weaknes:
- Invisibility countered by detection items and abilities
- Low base survivability requiring defensive items
- Minimal team utility beyond tracking and vision
- Movement speed bonus now tied to invisibility uptime rather than Track, reducing chase potential when revealed
- Physical damage scaling dependent on gold investment

core_mecanics:
- Big Game Hunter innate grants 15% bonus kill/assist gold from top 3 enemy net worth heroes, with a visible debuff marking them for Bounty Hunter and allies
- Track applies True Sight, damage amplification, vision, and generates bonus gold
- Shadow Walk provides invisibility with stun on first attack breaking stealth and 8/12/16/20% bonus movement speed while active
- Jinada autocast grants bonus damage and gold steal (15/22/29/36) on cooldown
- Shuriken Toss deals burst damage with chain bounce targeting
- Innate passives generate gold through kills, assists, and ability casts

strenges:
- Exceptional mobility through Shukuchi invisibility with near-instant activation
- Geminate Attack provides burst damage with automatic double attacks and applies all attack modifiers
- The Swarm grants vision and armor reduction across the map
- Time Lapse acts as an ultimate defensive reset for position, health, and mana
- Rewoven stacks attack range on ability cast, trading mobility for damage scaling

weaknes:
- Extremely fragile with low base stats, making positioning critical
- High mana dependency with limited mana pool constrains ability usage
- No hard crowd control, limiting solo kill potential without team support
- Swarm beetles die quickly, reducing their effectiveness

core_mecanics:
- Shukuchi: Invisibility mobility ability with AoE damage on activation
- Geminate Attack: Passive that grants a free second attack with bonus damage, applies attack effects
- The Swarm: Deploys a swarm of beetles that latch onto enemies, reducing armor and providing vision
- Time Lapse: Ultimate that reverts Weaver to position, HP, and mana from several seconds earlier
- Rewoven: Innate stack effect increasing attack range with each ability cast, offering choice between mobility (clearing stacks) and damage (retaining stacks)

strenges:
- Strong lane dominance through double attack projectiles
- Comprehensive crowd control with fire damage/burn and ice slow/stun
- Flexible auto-cast attack modifiers for varied combat scenarios
- Improved talent scaling with stronger Ice Path damage and Dual Breath cooldown reduction

weaknes:
- Zero mobility with no escape mechanisms
- High mana dependency limits sustain and ability usage; Liquid Fire and Liquid Frost now carry a 20 mana cost each
- Strict positioning requirements for ultimate effectiveness

core_mecanics:
- Double Trouble passive launches two projectiles with scaling damage reduction (51% minus 1% per level)
- Fire abilities (Dual Breath, Macropyre) provide damage-over-time
- Ice abilities (Ice Path) deliver crowd control and area control
- Auto-cast modifiers on Liquid Fire and Liquid Frost alter attack properties but cost 20 mana per use
- Aghanim's Shard removes the mana cost on Liquid Fire and Liquid Frost
- Facets (Twin Terror, Ice Breaker) provide distinct playstyle variations

strenges:
- Exceptional terrain mobility from Firefly, bypassing obstacles while providing zone control, vision, and an increasing movement speed bonus
- Powerful stacking debuff with Sticky Napalm, progressively slowing enemies and amplifying damage from all sources
- Versatile crowd control toolkit combining displacement, dragging, and channeled disable potential
- Facet system provides distinct playstyle options for different strategic approaches
- Revamped talent tree deepens commitment to core abilities, offering scaling options for Firefly, Sticky Napalm, Smoldering Resin, and Lasso

weaknes:
- Heavy mana dependency limits ability spam and sustained pressure
- Poor wave clear and farming speed delays item acquisition
- Lasso requires close-range initiation and breaks easily to crowd control
- Low base defensive stats make him vulnerable when caught without positioning advantage

core_mecanics:
- Firefly enables unhindered terrain traversal over extended duration while igniting and destroying trees, now granting an increasing movement speed bonus up to 12/18/24/30% by the end of the duration
- Sticky Napalm stacks amplify subsequent damage and progressively slow enemy movement and turn rate
- Flaming Lasso is a channeled disable that drags enemies toward Batrider
- Flamebreak provides AOE displacement with lingering damage
- Smoldering Resin applies passive damage-over-time on auto-attacks
- Aghanim's Scepter upgrades to double Lasso, Shard removes Napalm stack limit
- Talent highlights: +5% Firefly Max Movement Speed Bonus (10), +1s Smoldering Resin Duration (10), +0.5% Sticky Napalm Movement Slow (15), +30 Firefly DPS (15), Attacks apply 1 Stack of Sticky Napalm (20), +0.75s Flaming Lasso Duration (25)

strenges:
- Versatile creep control with five distinct aspects providing varied tactical utilities
- Global Hand of God enables massive team-wide healing and sustain
- Divine Favor aura provides team regeneration, heal amplification, and armor
- Generates strategic gold economy from controlled creep bounties
- Zealot innate provides a persistent companion creep with Martyrdom for burst damage or ally healing
- Holy Persuasion can globally unsummon undamaged creeps for flexible army management
- Holy Persuasion boosts all outgoing damage of controlled creeps, not just attack damage

weaknes:
- Six-second delay for creep teleportation limits repositioning flexibility
- High mana dependency constrains ability usage and sustain
- Complex creep management requires significant micro-management, increased by Zealot control
- Vulnerable to disruption with long cooldowns and fragile creeps
- Zealot has a 60-second respawn timer, leaving Chen without his companion if it dies

core_mecanics:
- Holy Persuasion controls multiple creeps with enhanced stats and increases all outgoing damage by 0/6/12/18%; can unsummon undamaged creeps globally at no mana cost
- Divine Favor aura provides regeneration, heal amp, and armor
- Hand of God delivers global healing to allies and creeps
- Penitence slows enemies while boosting ally attack speed against debuffed targets
- Five unique creep aspects provide diverse utilities
- Zealot innate spawns a melee creep on Chen's respawn that scales with game time and lane creep tier; Martyrdom lets it sacrifice itself to damage an enemy or heal an ally
- Creep teleportation is now cast via Zealot ability with 50 mana cost and 10s cooldown; self-targeting teleports all controlled units

strenges:
- Haunt provides global presence with illusions and vision
- Spectral Dagger enables terrain pathing and farming acceleration
- Desolate deals bonus pure damage against isolated targets
- Dispersion reflects received damage to nearby enemies
- Strong late-game scaling with item dependency
- Shadow Step offers frequent targeted gap-close with improved range and durable illusions
- Improved base movement speed and HP regen bolster survivability

weaknes:
- Extremely weak early game laning and farming
- Cooldown and mana limitations constrain ability usage
- Desolate positional requirement disables damage with nearby allies
- No built-in crowd control or dispel capabilities
- Slow farming speed without item investments
- Desolate base damage slightly reduced at early levels
- Haunt illusion damage lower at max rank, reducing late-game burst from illusions

core_mecanics:
- Illusion-based global mobility with Reality swapping capability
- Terrain traversal through Spectral Dagger paths
- Isolation damage mechanic requiring enemy separation
- Proximity-based damage reduction and reflection
- Farming accelerator dependency for power spike timing
- Shadow Step as short-cooldown targeted engage tool with scaling illusion survivability
- Haunt duration normalized to 6 seconds across all ranks with lower early cooldown

strenges:
- Exceptional casting range on all abilities
- Complete healing denial and execute mechanic with Ice Blast
- Magic damage amplification from Ice Vortex
- Strong lane control through constant harassment and vision
- Bone Chill innate stacks movement slow and Strength reduction from all ability damage, scaling with level
- All abilities now deal damage per second, significantly increasing sustained magic damage output
- Reduced Chilling Touch mana cost improves laning and spell sustain

weaknes:
- Slow movement speed and no escape
- No reliable disable - Cold Feet is conditional
- Severely countered by dispel and magic immunity
- Mana intensive with strict positioning requirements

core_mecanics:
- Bone Chill: innate passive — magic damage from abilities applies stacking 2% move slow and Strength reduction (0.1 + 0.1 per 3 levels) for 4s per stack; Aghanim's Scepter increases base Strength reduction by 0.3
- Cold Feet: conditional stun when enemy remains stationary within area; now deals 20/40/60/80 damage per second
- Ice Vortex: AoE slow that reduces magic resistance and provides vision; now also deals 10/20/30/40 damage per second and slows movement by 8%
- Chilling Touch: extended range attack modifier with slow; mana cost reduced to 35
- Ice Blast: global healing denial with frost stacks and low HP execute; now deals 12/24/36 damage per second
- Requires team coordination to hold enemies in Cold Feet
- Specialized counter to healing-reliant lineups
- Bone Chill Strength reduction compounds in prolonged fights, weakening enemy HP pools and regen

strenges:
- Doom ultimate completely neutralizes enemies with Mute and heal prevention; Aghanim's Scepter now also applies Break
- Self-cast Doom with Aghanim's creates walking disable aura
- Devour provides instant farming, ability theft, and gold generation on a shorter 66s cooldown
- High strength grants natural tankiness, amplified by Scorched Earth bonus health regen
- LVL ? Pain adds AoE burst damage in teamfights based on damage dealt to cursed target
- Aghanim's Shard Devour grants charges, Ancient creep devouring, and 40% Spell Amplification on stolen abilities
- Level 25 Permanent Scorched Earth provides constant movement, damage, and regen aura

weaknes:
- No natural mobility, requires Blink Dagger
- Slow casting ultimate with short range gives enemies reaction time
- Farm-dependent with long cooldowns creating vulnerability windows
- LVL ? Pain AoE is small (66 radius) and requires Doom to attack, limiting initiation patterns

core_mecanics:
- Disable key enemies with Doom ultimate
- Farm quickly and steal abilities with Devour
- Position with Scorched Earth mobility and health regen sustain
- Target tanky enemies with Infernal Blade percentage HP damage
- Apply LVL ? Pain curse on attack to create AoE burst; prioritize targets whose level is a multiple of 6 for 66% bonus damage and radius
- With Aghanim's Shard, devour Ancient creeps for powerful stolen abilities with bonus AoE and Spell Amplification

strenges:
- Exceptional single-target burst through stacking damage mechanic
- Massive attack speed buff enables prolonged fight dominance
- High survivability via damage reduction, debuff purge, and status resistance
- Passive damage scaling based on current health

weaknes:
- Vulnerable to kiting due to limited mobility
- Damage bonuses lost when not consistently attacking same target
- Performance dependent on specific item choices and mana regeneration
- Weak against multiple targets due to single-target focused design

core_mecanics:
- Fury Swipes: Passive damage amplification from consecutive attacks on same target
- Overpower: Fixed attack speed buff for rapid stack application
- Enrage: Instant debuff cleanse with damage reduction and status resistance
- Earthshock: AoE leap with damage and slow
- Maul: Health-to-damage conversion based on current HP

strenges:
- Unmatched global mobility to any visible target creating constant map pressure
- Strong synergy with global allies and mobility-based teammates
- Powerful crowd control through multiple stun mechanisms
- Effective at all game stages when properly supported
- Forces enemies to react constantly through psychological pressure
- Empowering Haste stacking allows exceeding the movement speed cap, snowballing speed in prolonged fights
- Greater Bash with Aghanim's Scepter enables chain-bashing multiple enemies through knockback collisions

weaknes:
- Severe early game limitations with weak laning presence
- Becomes functionally useless after using abilities in teamfights
- Highly predictable with straightforward mechanics at higher skill levels
- Dependent on specific items for core functionality; Planar Pocket now requires Aghanim's Shard investment
- Mana constraints limit ability uptime and sustain
- Planar Pocket significantly weaker with reduced magic resistance and 900 unit range leash

core_mecanics:
- Empowering Haste innate grants stacking movement speed on stunning enemies (8%/2s for heroes, 2%/1s for creeps), can exceed the speed cap
- Charge of Darkness provides global initiation and path-stunning capability
- Greater Bash passively bashes with damage scaling from movement speed; Aghanim's Scepter adds knockback collision bashing and bonus damage on impact
- Planar Pocket (Aghanim's Shard) redirects enemy spells to self with 40% magic resistance, ends beyond 900 units but castable during Charge of Darkness
- Nether Strike executes pick-off bursts with short cast range

strenges:

- Strong multi-target damage output through Flak Cannon
- Reliable pickoff potential with Homing Missile (lock-on projectile, damage, stun)
- Potent burst damage combo with Rocket Barrage and Call Down
- Excellent laning phase advantage
- Exceptional very late game scaling with critical strike items
- Afterburner innate grants ramping movement speed in fights, rewarding sustained aggression on heroes and creeps

weaknes:

- Poor mid-late game performance slump before ultra late game
- Homing Missile can be destroyed by enemies before impact
- Limited range on spells (missile and rockets are short-medium range)
- High mana costs in early-mid game
- Dependent on expensive core items to reach full effectiveness

- Solar Guardian global ultimate with damage, heal, and landing stun; now creates 6 second daytime on cast to activate Break of Dawn bonuses
- Luminosity passive that scales with attacks for crit and ally healing
- Break of Dawn grants max bonuses during any daytime caused by Dawnbreaker's own abilities (including Solar Guardian)
- Talent rework: level 10 offers Celestial Hammer trail ally movement speed or +25% Luminosity crit damage; level 15 offers increased Solar Guardian CDR (20s) or +40% Celestial Hammer trail/hammer damage

strenges:

- High mobility with terrain-crossing and repositioning capabilities through Rebound, including alt-cast to bring an ally to the destination
- Chainable crowd control with multiple disable types (displacement, stun, slow) that prevent immunity counterplay
- Strong ally synergy via Bodyguard with shared damage barrier and 75% of passive bonuses (lifesteal and bonus base attack damage) granted to target
- Permanent self-buffing through Bodyguard passive granting lifesteal and bonus base attack damage without mana cost
- Dominant early-mid game power with peak effectiveness before 25 minutes
- Strong initiation and defensive escape options from versatile mobility
- Barrier sustain in fights through hero attacks restoring 30% of damage dealt to the shared barrier

weaknes:

- Mana constraints limit sustained fight presence and full combo execution
- Short range vulnerability to kiting from ranged opponents and zoning capabilities
- High positioning and execution skill requirement with severe punishment for mistakes
- Significant late game power drop-off with rapidly diminishing impact beyond 35 minutes
- Bodyguard barrier and bonuses vulnerable to dispels — dispelling Marci removes the barrier, dispelling the ally removes both barrier and passive bonuses

core_mecanics:

- Physical brawler playstyle requiring close quarters engagement for maximum effectiveness
- Combo flow of Dispose (displacement) → Rebound (stun/position) → Bodyguard barrier on ally → ultimate for sustained fight
- Bodyguard dual-mode: passive self-buff (lifesteal + bonus base damage) always active; castable on ally for 75% of bonuses plus a shared damage barrier (7s duration, 20s cooldown)
- Early-mid game focused strategy, aiming to close out games during peak power windows
- Position-dependent mobility where Rebound targets determine initiation vs escape options; alt-cast variant repositions an ally alongside Marci
- Special Delivery calls Marci's courier to teleport to her, automatically grabbing stash items from fountain before arrival
- Level 25 talent option for Bodyguard Strong Dispel on both Marci and the target enhances late-game utility

strenges:
- Global engagement capability with highly impactful crowd control on Onslaught
- Natural tankiness scaling through Colossal granting increasing AoE and Slow Resistance per Max Health
- Skirmish disruptor through sustained AoE damage while mobile with Trample
- Becomes stronger as fights continue via Uproar stacking stats and area slow
- Powerful channeled lockdown with Pulverize that creates repeated team fight disruption
- Level 25 talent doubles Colossal bonuses during Trample for massive AoE and near-immunity to slows

weaknes:
- Self-disarmed during Trample, limiting fighting capability while ability active
- Highly positional dependent with short ult range and commitment-based charge
- Vulnerable to mobility disparity when abilities are on cooldown
- Pathing reliant with abilities that can be body blocked or juked
- All utility requires successful skill execution rather than passive reliability
- Pulverize AoE slightly reduced, demanding tighter positioning for full team fight impact

core_mecanics:
- Colossal provides 10% base Slow Resistance and grants +0.5% AoE and +1% Slow Resistance per 100 Max Health
- Onslaught charges globally to target location, stunning and knocking enemies on impact
- Trample damages enemies around Primal Beast per distance traveled while self-disarmed
- Uproar stacks on damage taken to grant increasing attack damage, armor, and enemy slow aura
- Pulverize is a channeled ult that grabs a target and repeatedly slams them into the ground with 575 AoE radius

strenges:

- Physical damage immunity during ultimate creates safe window for aggression
- Pierce the Veil passive enables attacking ethereal targets and dealing magical damage, bypassing common defenses
- High burst and sustained damage potential through Gunslinger multi-hit mechanics
- Long-range skillshot control with Dead Shot provides lane dominance and poke
- Zone control and teamfight presence with The Calling revenants
- Supernatural spell amplification stacking on nearby hero kills rewards aggressive teamfight presence and snowballing
- Pierce the Veil now grants +75% base damage, scaling harder with items than flat bonus
- Spectral Slug (Shard) adds knockback, ethereal forcing, and magic vulnerability to kit, expanding utility

weaknes:

- Cannot damage magic immune targets during ultimate (critical vulnerability)
- No innate mobility or escape mechanisms, positioning dependent
- Skillshot abilities require aim and timing, can be avoided
- Reliance on attack damage and attack speed for effectiveness
- Melee-like weaknesses in lane despite being ranged hero
- Losing half Supernatural stacks on death heavily punishes poor positioning or trades
- Level 15 talent no longer provides +55 Gunslinger damage, reducing mid-game burst scaling

core_mecanics:

- Supernatural: Passive granting 1% spell amplification per nearby enemy hero death, stacking up to current level; loses half stacks on death
- Dead Shot: Line skillshot that ricochets and fears, primary poke and engage tool
- The Calling: AoE revenant summon providing slow, silence, and damage zone
- Gunslinger: Toggle granting chance for second attack on nearby targets, core damage amplifier
- Pierce the Veil: Transformation ultimate converting all attack damage to magical, granting physical damage immunity and +75% base damage; passive component allows attacking ethereal targets and converts attack damage to magical when either Muerta or target is ethereal, with physical lifesteal treated as spell lifesteal
- Spectral Slug (Aghanim's Shard): Fires a spectral bullet dealing 225 damage, knocking back 250 units, and turning the target ethereal for 3s with 30% slow and 20% magic damage vulnerability; 500 range, 75 mana, 12s cooldown

strenges:
- Flexible melee hero with dual weapon systems and adaptive ability sets
- Multiple combat styles available through weapon switching mechanics
- High skill expression through complex ability combinations
- Strong potential for creative playstyles with versatile toolkit
- Switch Discipline now grants 12% bonus damage on first Katana hit/ability and +12% movement speed for 2s after switching to Sai, rewarding frequent weapon swaps
- Shodo Sai no longer restricts passive Bash procs, enabling Skull Basher and Abyssal Blade synergy
- Mark stun duration now scales (0.5/0.6/0.7/0.8s), stronger in late game than before

weaknes:
- Complex kit may have steep learning curve
- Reduced base movement speed (310) and attack speed (100) lower early-game trading power
- Talon Toss range heavily reduced (650/750/850/950), limiting early initiation and catch potential
- Raptor Dance no longer provides magic damage immunity, making Kez vulnerable to magic burst during the ultimate

- Aghanim's Scepter no longer restarts alternate ability cooldown if already on cooldown, reducing Scepter flexibility

core_mecanics:
- Dual weapon system enabling different combat approaches
- Weapon switching creates adaptation opportunities with distinct bonuses: Katana grants burst damage, Sai grants mobility
- Complex ability interactions requiring mastery
- Versatile toolkit for multiple strategic situations
- Shodo Sai proc reworked: 20% Critical Strike Chance replaces mark application; marks now exclusive to parry and Raven's Veil
- Aghanim's Shard upgrade for Shodo Sai: parrying creates a stronger mark with +0.2s stun and 50% crit bonus
- Grappling Claw always destroys the targeted tree and ends at the tree's position
